source: PinnerSage- Multi-Modal User Embedding Framework for Recommendations at Pinterest

concepts.finding: PinnerSage: user embedding as a multi-set of "pins" (interaction items) via hierarchical clustering (Ward's method). Represents user by multiple centroids rather than single vector. Each centroid captures a distinct interest cluster. Clustering+aggregation over user interaction history produces rich user representation. 6% engagement improvement online.
concepts.implication: Keyword multi-representation: each keyword belongs to multiple "intent clusters" via soft clustering. Keyword embedding as multi-set of cluster centroids rather than single vector. Each centroid captures different intent facet of the keyword.
concepts.open_question: Should keywords be represented as single intent or multiple intents with strength scores? How many intent clusters per keyword?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Multi-centroid keyword representation (PinnerSage-style, each centroid = one intent) captures multi-intent keywords better than single-vector embedding approaches.
